figures.caption: Table 2. Main comparison on Acc@1 (%) against SSL baselines. SR and V-MAE denote SurgRec and VideoMAE, respectively. SR-MAE (w/o bal.) refers to SurgRecMAE trained without balanced sampling. Dataset names are abbreviated (e.g., Cat: Cataract, Colono: Colonoscopic).
Table 3. VLM Acc@1 (%). SurgRec-MAE is the supervised reference. P1: baseline prompt; P2: stricter output constraint. ∆ = P2−P1 (points). Dataset names are abbreviated as in Table 2.
Table 1. Pretraining data scale comparison across recent medical foundation models.
Fig. 1. Overview of the proposed medical video pretraining and evaluation framework.
Table 4. Generalization analysis on the 4-domain benchmark (Acc@1, %). Domain scores are macro-averaged; Overall Macro and Worst Domain denote the mean and minimum across domains, respectively.
